# airtelcloud_lb_virtual_server (Resource)

Manages a Virtual Server on an Airtel Cloud Load Balancer Service. A virtual server defines L4/L7 load balancing rules with backend nodes.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `interval` (Number) The health check interval in seconds.
- `lb_service_id` (String) The ID of the parent LB service.
- `nodes` (Attributes List) The backend nodes for the virtual server. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--nodes))
- `port` (Number) The listener port number.
- `protocol` (String) The listener protocol (HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, UDP).
- `routing_algorithm` (String) The load balancing algorithm (e.g., ROUND_ROBIN, LEAST_CONNECTIONS).
- `vip_port_id` (Number) The VIP port ID to bind this virtual server to.
- `vpc_id` (String) The VPC ID.

### Optional

- `certificate_id` (String) The SSL certificate ID (required for HTTPS protocol).
- `monitor_protocol` (String) The health check monitor protocol (HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, UDP).
- `name` (String) The name of the virtual server.
- `persistence_enabled` (Boolean) Whether session persistence is enabled.
- `persistence_type` (String) The session persistence type (cookie or source_ip).
- `redirect_https` (Boolean) Whether to redirect HTTP to HTTPS.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))
- `x_forwarded_for` (Boolean) Whether to add X-Forwarded-For header.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the virtual server.
- `status` (String) The current status of the virtual server.
- `vip` (String) The virtual IP address.

<a id="nestedatt--nodes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `nodes`

Required:

- `compute_id` (Number) The compute instance ID.
- `compute_ip` (String) The compute instance IP address.
- `port` (Number) The backend port number.

Optional:

- `max_conn` (Number) The maximum connections for this node.
- `weight` (Number) The weight for weighted load balancing.


<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
